title: Interpret multiplying fraction and whole number word problems
description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number.  Then interpret the solution by, for example, determining between which two whole numbers the solution falls.
topic_path: Module 5: Fraction equivalence, ordering, and operations
Topic G: Repeated addition of fractions as multiplication
Interpret multiplying fraction and whole number word problems

assessment_items.question_content:
Over a one week period each person in the Taylor family eats $\dfrac34$ of a box of cereal. There are  $5$ people in the family.

**How many boxes of cereal does the Taylor family eat in one week?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each person eats $\maroonD{\dfrac34}$ of a box of cereal.


[[☃ image 1]]

There are  $\blueD{5}$ people in the family who eat cereal.


[[☃ image 1]]



We can multiply $\blueD{5 \times}\maroonD{\dfrac34}$ to figure out how many boxes of cereal the Taylor family needs for one week.
$\blueD{5 \times} \maroonD{\dfrac34}=\dfrac{\blueD{5 \times}\maroonD3} {\maroonD4}={\dfrac{15}{4}}$
The Taylor family needs between $3$ and $4$ boxes of cereal for one week.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leonardo uses $\dfrac23$ of a bottle of green paint to paint each picture of a forest.  He plans to paint $5$ pictures of forests.

**How many bottles of green paint will Leonardo use?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each picture requires $\green{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ of a bottle of green paint. Leonardo plans to paint $\blue{5}$ pictures.

Picture $\blue1$ | Picture $\blue2$  | Picture $\blue3$ |Picture $\blue4$ | Picture $\blue5$
:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: 
![Rectangle divided into three equal sized parts with two parts shaded green. Rectangle is labeled two-thirds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a16e13c8bebdeba3d7865f8c5812e2cf7c3d130)|![Rectangle divided into three equal sized parts with two parts shaded green. Rectangle is labeled two-thirds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a16e13c8bebdeba3d7865f8c5812e2cf7c3d130)|![Rectangle divided into three equal sized parts with two parts shaded green. Rectangle is labeled two-thirds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a16e13c8bebdeba3d7865f8c5812e2cf7c3d130)|![Rectangle divided into three equal sized parts with two parts shaded green. Rectangle is labeled two-thirds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a16e13c8bebdeba3d7865f8c5812e2cf7c3d130)|![Rectangle divided into three equal sized parts with two parts shaded green. Rectangle is labeled two-thirds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a16e13c8bebdeba3d7865f8c5812e2cf7c3d130)

We can multiply $\blue{5} \times\green{\dfrac23}$ to figure out how many bottles of green paint he will use.

$\quad{\blue{5} \times} \green{\dfrac23}$

$=\dfrac{{\blue{5} \times }\:\green 2} {\green3}$

$={\dfrac{10}{3}}$
Leonardo will use between $3$ and $4$ bottles of green paint.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $6$ people in Eden's family. Each person drinks $\dfrac25$ of a liter of milk each week.

**How many liters of milk does Eden's family drink each week?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each person drinks $\maroonD{\dfrac25}$ of a liter each week. We want to know how much $\blueD6$ people drink. 


Person $\blueD1$ | Person $\blueD2$  | Person $\blueD3$ |Person $\blueD4$ | Person $\blueD5$ | Person $\blueD6$
:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: 
![Rectangle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded pink. Rectangle is labeled two-fifths. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d558ae02c820782b286ba8b290428522a041aa1)|![Rectangle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded pink. Rectangle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d558ae02c820782b286ba8b290428522a041aa1)|![Rectangle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded pink. Rectangle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d558ae02c820782b286ba8b290428522a041aa1)|![Rectangle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded pink. Rectangle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d558ae02c820782b286ba8b290428522a041aa1)|![Rectangle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded pink. Rectangle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d558ae02c820782b286ba8b290428522a041aa1)|![Rectangle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded pink. Rectangle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d558ae02c820782b286ba8b290428522a041aa1)

We can multiply $\blueD6 \times\maroonD{\dfrac25}$ .
Eden's family drinks between $2$ and $3$ liters of milk each week.

assessment_items.question_content:
A salmon fish eats $\dfrac32$ kilograms of krill in one week. 

**How many kilograms of krill would a salmon eat in $3$ weeks?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A salmon fish eats $\maroonD{\dfrac32}$ kilograms of krill in one week.



[[☃ image 1]]

We want to find how many kilograms of krill a salmon fish eats in  $\blueD{3}$ weeks.


[[☃ image 1]]



We can multiply $\blueD{3 \times}\maroonD{\dfrac32}$ to figure out how many kilograms of krill a salmon eats in $\blueD3$ weeks.
$\blueD{3 \times} \maroonD{\dfrac32}=\dfrac{\blueD{3 \times}\maroonD3} {\maroonD2}={\dfrac{9}{2}}$
A salmon will eat between  $4$ and $5$ kilograms of krill in $3$ weeks.

assessment_items.question_content:
For a science experiment, each group is given $\dfrac{11}8$ liters of purple liquid. 

**How many liters are needed for $3$ groups?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each group gets $\purpleD{\dfrac{11}8}$ liters of purple liquid. We want to know the total amount of liquid for $\blueD3$ groups.

Group $\blueD1$ | Group $\blueD2$  | Group $\blueD3$ 
:-: |:-: |:-: 
![A rectangle divided into eight equal parts with all parts shaded purple. Under this rectangle is the label eight-eighths. Above this rectangle is the label Group 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/00b74fe7aa302cf79c9dbe1e801878d9250a76a5)![A rectangle divided into eight equal parts with three parts shaded purple. Under the rectangle is the label three-eighths. It is also under the label of Group 1. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c640cb9f1f0c54ae177bee7e11be5167a4d22476)| ![A rectangle divided into eight equal parts with all parts shaded purple. Under this rectangle is the label eight-eighths. Above this rectangle is the label Group 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/00b74fe7aa302cf79c9dbe1e801878d9250a76a5)![A rectangle divided into eight equal parts with three parts shaded purple. Under the rectangle is the label three-eighths. It is also under the label of Group 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c640cb9f1f0c54ae177bee7e11be5167a4d22476) | ![A rectangle divided into eight equal parts with all parts shaded purple. Under this rectangle is the label eight-eighths. Above this rectangle is the label Group 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/00b74fe7aa302cf79c9dbe1e801878d9250a76a5)![A rectangle divided into eight equal parts with three parts shaded purple. Under the rectangle is the label three-eighths. It is also under the label of Group 3. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c640cb9f1f0c54ae177bee7e11be5167a4d22476) 

We can multiply $\blueD{3} \times\purpleD{\dfrac{11}8}$ to figure out how many liters of purple liquid are needed.
Between  $4$ and $5$ liters of purple liquid are needed for $3$ groups.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leah knit a scarf that is $130$ centimeters $(\text{cm})$ long.  Theo knit a scarf that is $\dfrac{4}{3}$ as long as Leah's scarf.

**How long is Theo's scarf?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Leah's scarf is $\purpleD{130\,\text{cm}}$ long.


[[☃ image 1]]

Theo's scarf is $\dfrac{4}{3}$ as long.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can multiply $\purpleD{130}\times \dfrac{4}{3}$ to find out how long Theo's scarf is.
$=\greenE{173\dfrac{1}{3}}$
Theo's scarf is between $173\,\text{cm}$ and $174\,\text{cm}$ long.

assessment_items.question_content:
Marcus bought a rug that is  $6$ meters $(\text{m})$ long.  Vinny bought a rug that is $\dfrac{3}{4}$ as long as Marcus's rug.

**How long is Vinny's rug?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Marcus's rug is $\purpleD{6\,\text{m}}$ long.



[[☃ image 1]]

Vinny's rug is $\dfrac{3}{4}$ as long.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can multiply $\purpleD{6}\times \dfrac{3}{4}$ to find out how long Vinny's rug is.
$=\greenE{4\dfrac{1}{2}}$
Vinny's rug is between $4\,\text{m}$ and $5\,\text{m}$ long.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charles biked  $\dfrac23$ of a kilometer yesterday. He biked $7$ times as far today.

**How many kilometers did Charles bike today?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Charles biked $\maroonD{\dfrac23}$ of a kilometer yesterday.


[[☃ image 1]]

He biked $\blueD{7}$ times as far today.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can multiply $\blueD{7 \times}\maroonD{\dfrac23}$ to figure out how far Charles biked today.
Charles biked between $4$ and $5$ kilometers today.

assessment_items.question_content:
$5$ friends each have $\dfrac34$ of the money needed to a buy a bag of popcorn at the movie theatre.

**What is the largest number of bags they can buy?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blue5$ people. Each person  has $\green{{\dfrac34}}$ of the money needed for one bag.

Person $\blue1$ | Person $\blue2$  | Person $\blue3$ | Person $\blue4$ | Person $\blue5$ 
:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: 
![Rectangle divided  into four equal-sized parts with three parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled three-fourths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45e8f559914343d19877fa782f50fecd2f93a21f) |![Rectangle divided  into four equal-sized parts with three parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled three-fourths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45e8f559914343d19877fa782f50fecd2f93a21f) |![Rectangle divided  into four equal-sized parts with three parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled three-fourths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45e8f559914343d19877fa782f50fecd2f93a21f) |![Rectangle divided  into four equal-sized parts with three parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled three-fourths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45e8f559914343d19877fa782f50fecd2f93a21f) |![Rectangle divided  into four equal-sized parts with three parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled three-fourths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45e8f559914343d19877fa782f50fecd2f93a21f) 

We can multiply $\blue5 \times\green{\dfrac34}$ to figure out how many bags they can buy.

$\quad{\blue5 \times} \green{\dfrac34}$

$=\dfrac{{\blue5 \times }\:\green 3} {\green4}$

$={\dfrac{15}{4}}$

$={3\dfrac{3}{4}}$

![A fraction bar that represents one whole. it is shaded green with the label of 1 below it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eb77f7f3183b65d6fb9d87b9cacfaf085c9e540d)![A fraction bar that represents one whole. it is shaded green with the label of 1 below it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eb77f7f3183b65d6fb9d87b9cacfaf085c9e540d)![A fraction bar that represents one whole. it is shaded green with the label of 1 below it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eb77f7f3183b65d6fb9d87b9cacfaf085c9e540d)![A fraction bar divided into four equal sized pieces with three pieces shaded green. A red X is on top of the bar. Below is the fraction three-fourths written in red.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ab4bd505b9485a2afa84c697c3f1005a96345fb)

Since they cannot buy $\red{\dfrac34}$ of a bag, and they do not have enough money to buy $4$ bags. 

So they can buy only $3$ bags.
The largest number they can buy is $3$ bags of popcorn.

assessment_items.question_content:
$6$ friends are at a pizzeria. They want to order enough pizza so that each person can eat at least $\dfrac25$ of a pizza.

**What is the least number of pizzas the friends should order?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blue6$ people. Each person wants to eat at least $\red{\dfrac25}$ of a pizza.

Person $\blue1$ | Person $\blue2$ | Person $\blue3$ | Person $\blue4$ |   Person $\blue5$ |  Person $\blue6$    
:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: 
![Circle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded. Circle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/004c803c351b07b770f4e88282bc460dfc3346a5)| ![Circle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded. Circle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/004c803c351b07b770f4e88282bc460dfc3346a5) | ![Circle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded. Circle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/004c803c351b07b770f4e88282bc460dfc3346a5)|![Circle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded. Circle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/004c803c351b07b770f4e88282bc460dfc3346a5)|![Circle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded. Circle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/004c803c351b07b770f4e88282bc460dfc3346a5)|![Circle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded. Circle is labeled two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/004c803c351b07b770f4e88282bc460dfc3346a5)

We can multiply $\blue6 \times\red{\dfrac25}$ to figure out how many pizzas they should order.

$\quad{\blue6 \times} \red{\dfrac25}$

$=\dfrac{{\blue6 \times }\:\red 2} {\red5}$

$={\dfrac{12}{5}}$

$={2\dfrac{2}{5}}$

![A circle divided into five equal parts with all parts shaded red. Under the circle is the label 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/052e2ab75e7f512fa0acbe5b9db44fbbc35cb1df)![A circle divided into five equal parts with all parts shaded red. Under the circle is the label 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/052e2ab75e7f512fa0acbe5b9db44fbbc35cb1df)![A circle divided into five equal parts with two parts shaded red. Under the circle is the label two-fifths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/004c803c351b07b770f4e88282bc460dfc3346a5)

They cannot order $\dfrac25$ of a pizza, and they will not have enough for everyone if they only order $2$ pizzas.

So they must order $3$ whole pizzas.
The least number of pizzas they should order is $3$ pizzas.

assessment_items.question_content:
There will be $6$ people at Ellie's pie party. She wants to bake enough pie so that each person can eat at least $\dfrac15$ of a pie. 

**What is the least number of pies Ellie should bake?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blue6$ people. Each person will eat at least $\red{\dfrac15}$ of a pie.

Person $\blue1$ | Person $\blue2$ | Person $\blue3$ | Person $\blue4$ | Person $\blue5$  | Person $\blue6$  
:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: 
![Circle divided into five equal parts with one part shaded. Circle is labeled one-fifth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36694e804229eba05219eb049c7bf2f7eb1fd243)| ![Circle divided into five equal parts with one part shaded. Circle is labeled one-fifth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36694e804229eba05219eb049c7bf2f7eb1fd243) | ![Circle divided into five equal parts with one part shaded. Circle is labeled one-fifth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36694e804229eba05219eb049c7bf2f7eb1fd243)|![Circle divided into five equal parts with one part shaded. Circle is labeled one-fifth](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36694e804229eba05219eb049c7bf2f7eb1fd243)|![Circle divided into five equal parts with one part shaded. Circle is labeled one-fifth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36694e804229eba05219eb049c7bf2f7eb1fd243)|![Circle divided into five equal parts with one part shaded. Circle is labeled one-fifth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36694e804229eba05219eb049c7bf2f7eb1fd243)
We can multiply $\blue6 \times\red{\dfrac15}$ to figure out how many pies Ellie should bake.

$\quad{\blue6 \times} \red{\dfrac15}$

$=\dfrac{{\blue6 \times }\:\red 1} {\red5}$

$={\dfrac{6}{5}}$

$={1\dfrac{1}{5}}$

![A circle divided into five equal parts with all parts shaded red. The label 1 appears under the circle in red.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9388c411d4ef7a483bf2ae3d4dd9975e79ae7a0a)![A circle divided into five equal parts with one part shaded red. The label of one-fifth in red is under the circle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36694e804229eba05219eb049c7bf2f7eb1fd243)

Ellie cannot bake $\dfrac15$ of a pie, and there will not be enough pie for everyone if she only bakes $1$ pie.

So Ellie must bake $2$ whole pies.
The least number of pies Ellie should bake is $2$ pies.

assessment_items.question_content:
Takeru has $4$ birdfeeders. It takes $\dfrac43$ bags of birdseed to fill each feeder.

**What is the fewest number of bags of birdseed that Takeru needs to fill all the feeders?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD4$ bird feeders. Each bird feeder holds $\greenD{\dfrac43}$ bags of birdseed.

Feeder $\blue1$ | Feeder $\blue2$  | Feeder $\blue3$ | Feeder $\blue4$ 
:-: |:-: |:-: |:-: 
![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all three parts shaded green. Below the bar is the label three-thirds. Above the bar is the label Feeder 1. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d53a2bdf86d20bb206a0be9a72d59c5c4e26178)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with one part shaded green. Under the bar is the label one-third. It is also under the label of Feeder 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f03589b479dc40edef62968bb5711dde3ac1370) | ![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all three parts shaded green. Below the bar is the label three-thirds. Above the bar is the label Feeder 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d53a2bdf86d20bb206a0be9a72d59c5c4e26178)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with one part shaded green. Under the bar is the label one-third. It is also under the label of Feeder 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f03589b479dc40edef62968bb5711dde3ac1370) | ![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all three parts shaded green. Below the bar is the label three-thirds. Above the bar is the label Feeder 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d53a2bdf86d20bb206a0be9a72d59c5c4e26178)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with one part shaded green. Under the bar is the label one-third. It is also under the label of Feeder 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f03589b479dc40edef62968bb5711dde3ac1370) | ![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all three parts shaded green. Below the bar is the label three-thirds. Above the bar is the label Feeder 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d53a2bdf86d20bb206a0be9a72d59c5c4e26178)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with one part shaded green. Under the bar is the label one-third. It is also under the label of Feeder 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f03589b479dc40edef62968bb5711dde3ac1370)

We can multiply $\blueD4 \times\greenD{\dfrac43}$ to figure out how many bags of birdseed Takeru needs to fill all the feeders.
${\blueD4 \times} \greenD{\dfrac43}=\dfrac{{\blueD4 \times }\:\greenD4} {\greenD3}={\dfrac{16}{3}}$

$={5\dfrac{1}{3}}$

![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all parts shaded green. Under the bar is the label 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d4e3834b3565fd2520d43f701f7977f80bc4847)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all parts shaded green. Under the bar is the label 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d4e3834b3565fd2520d43f701f7977f80bc4847)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all parts shaded green. Under the bar is the label 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d4e3834b3565fd2520d43f701f7977f80bc4847)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all parts shaded green. Under the bar is the label 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d4e3834b3565fd2520d43f701f7977f80bc4847)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with all parts shaded green. Under the bar is the label 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d4e3834b3565fd2520d43f701f7977f80bc4847)![A fraction bar divided into three equal parts with one part shaded green. Under the bar is the label one-third.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f03589b479dc40edef62968bb5711dde3ac1370)

Takeru cannot buy $\dfrac13$ of a bag of birdseed, and the feeders will not all be full if he only uses $5$ bags.

So Takeru must buy $6$ whole bags.
Takeru needs at least $6$ bags of birdseed.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dayton has $3$ bags of frozen  vegetables. Each bag is $\dfrac58$ full of vegetables. He plans to make vegetable soup. Each pot of soup takes $1$ full bag of vegetables. 
 
**How many full pots of soup can Dayton make?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD3$ bags of frozen vegetables. Each bag is $\greenD{\dfrac58}$ full of vegetables.

Bag $\blue1$ | Bag $\blue2$  | Bag $\blue3$ 
:-: |:-: |:-: 
![Rectangle divided into 8 equal parts with 5 parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled five-eighths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/db50e1454f8afe6cbff6d143f8057a9788acd39b) | ![Rectangle divided into 8 equal parts with 5 parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled five-eighths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/db50e1454f8afe6cbff6d143f8057a9788acd39b) | ![Rectangle divided into 8 equal parts with 5 parts shaded. Rectangle is labeled five-eighths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/db50e1454f8afe6cbff6d143f8057a9788acd39b) 

We can multiply $\blueD3 \times\green{\dfrac58}$ to figure out how many pots of soup Dayton can make.
${\blueD3 \times} \greenD{\dfrac58}=\dfrac{{\blueD3 \times }\:\greenD5} {\greenD8}={\dfrac{15}{8}}$

$={1\dfrac{7}{8}}$

![A fraction bar divided into eight equal-sized parts with all eight parts shaded green. The bar is labeled 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c15ed6e8eae7913531c95b85daab27abad1aa4ad)![A fraction bar divided into eight equal-sized parts with seven parts shaded green. A large red letter X is over the fraction bar. The bar is labeled seven-eighths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/33c21bdbcd824ab9fb83c86990345ae422f1b5c9)

Dayton cannot make a full pot of soup with $\dfrac78$ of a bag of vegetables. He can only make $1$ full pot of soup.
Dayton can make $1$ full pot of soup.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mina has $\dfrac{4}{5}$ of the flour that she needs to make $12$ rolls.

**What is the greatest number of rolls Mina can make with the flour she has?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Mina has $\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{5}}$ of the flour she needs for $\blueE{12}$ rolls.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can multiply $\blueE{12}\times \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{5}}$ to find out how many rolls Mina can make.
Since it doesn't make sense to make part of a roll, Mina can only make $9$ rolls with the flour she has.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jim has $\dfrac{7}{10}$ of the sugar that he needs to make $24$ cupcakes.

**What is the greatest number of cupcakes that Jim can make with the sugar he has?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Jim has $\purpleD{\dfrac{7}{10}}$ of the sugar he needs for $\blueE{24}$ cupcakes.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can multiply $\blueE{24}\times \purpleD{\dfrac{7}{10}}$ to find out how many cupcakes Jim can make.
Since it doesn't make sense to make part of a cupcake, Jim can only make $16$ cupcakes with the sugar he has.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nicole has $\dfrac{2}{3}$ of the butter she needs to make $14$ mini pies.

**What is the greatest number of mini pies that Nicole can make with the butter she has?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Nicole has $\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ of the butter she needs for $\blueE{14}$ mini pies.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can multiply $\blueE{14}\times \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ to find out how many mini pies Nicole can make.
$= 9\dfrac13$
Since it doesn't make sense to make part of a mini pie, the greatest number of mini pies Nicole can make is $9$.
